Category: Healthcare & Life Sciences: Medical Services, Pharmacy, Biotechnology, Hospitals
Who is this for: Doctors, Nurses, Physician Assistants, Nurse Practitioners, Clinical Staff, Healthcare Professionals, Medical Technicians, Pharmacists, Radiologists, Laboratory Technicians
Team or Dept this is for: Clinical Operations, Medical Staff, Nursing, Pharmacy, Quality Assurance, Patient Safety, Healthcare IT, Medical Affairs
Description: Why clinicians may over-trust AI outputs, and how errors happen.
Prerequisites: A basic familiarity with clinical workflows and a general awareness of how digital tools are used in healthcare settings is all that is needed to get started with this course.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the key causes of automation bias in clinical settings and explain how over-reliance on AI-generated outputs can lead to diagnostic and treatment errors. You will also apply practical strategies to critically evaluate AI recommendations, helping you maintain safe, patient-centred care in your everyday practice.
